lesson.menu_desc: Describe what was happening when something else interrupted — was/were + -ing with when and while.
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 — Past Continuous: Setting the Scene
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Daniela Feitoza | Aula 15 -- Past Continuous: Setting the Scene

listenings.text: Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Today, listen to one short voice note about the past continuous, the tense that sets the scene. Use it for a longer action that was already happening in the past. We were testing the new system at midnight. The team was watching the monitor. While the update was running, everything looked calm. Now, here is the key. When a short action interrupts the long one, the long action stays in the past continuous and the short action goes to the past simple. So, we were testing the system when the server crashed. While the team was watching, an alert appeared. Remember the form. Use was with I, he, she and it. Use were with you, we and they, plus the verb and i n g. Say I was testing, never I was test, and never I were testing. Use while before the long action and when before the short one. Try it. Describe a scene at work, what was happening, and the moment something interrupted it. Talk soon.

Hello Daniela, this is Marcus. I am leaving you a voicemail about last night. So, here is the scene. At eleven, the team was running the final update and I was monitoring the dashboard from home. Everything was working well. While the update was loading, the response time started to lag. Then, at eleven fifteen, an alert appeared on the screen. We were checking the logs when the whole store froze. A glitch in the payment step occurred, and the system went down for ten minutes. Luckily, our backup was running, so no data was lost. While I was rebooting the server, Carla was calling the vendor. By midnight, everything was stable again. I think we noticed the problem early because we were watching the monitor the whole time. Call me back when you can. Talk soon, Daniela.
